revision.text:
'''Koxinga Ancestral Shrine'''(鄭成功祖廟) was a [[family shrine]] built in [[Tainan City]] , [[Taiwan]], [[1663]] by [[Zheng Jin]], to worship his father [[Koxinga]].
When Taiwan became part of the [[Qing]] Empire, it was renamed "The Cheng's Ancestral Shrine"(鄭氏大宗祠) and today the official name is "Ancestral Shrine of Koxinga". The complex is traditional and elegant; an old well in front of the gate, the old well is all that remains of the original shrine. The cetral hall worships the statue of Koxinga as well as the spririt tablets of each generation of ancestors. In 1771, there was a famouse wooden tablot with the character "Three Generations Heritage"(三圭世錫) to prize the virtue of Koxinga's familly.
[[category:Taiwan]] [[zh:鄭成功祖廟]] [[ja:鄭成功祖廟]]

'''Koxinga Ancestral Shrine'''(鄭成功祖廟) was a [[family shrine]] built in [[Tainan City]] , [[Taiwan]], [[1663]] by [[Zheng Jin]], to worship his father [[Koxinga]].
When Taiwan became part of the [[Qing Dynasty|Qing Empire]], it was renamed "The Cheng's Ancestral Shrine"(鄭氏大宗祠) and today the official name is "Ancestral Shrine of Koxinga". The complex is traditional and elegant; an old well in front of the gate, the old well is all that remains of the original shrine.
The cetral hall worships the statue of Koxinga as well as the spririt tablets of each generation of ancestors. In 1771, there was a famouse wooden tablot with the character "Three Generations Heritage"(三圭世錫) to prize the virtue of Koxinga's familly.
In this shrine, there is also a sculpture of young Koinxga and his mother in Japanese clothes.
[[category:Taiwan]] [[zh:鄭成功祖廟]] [[ja:鄭成功祖廟]]

revision.comment: AWB general fixes and delink dates per [[:WP:DATELINK]], [[:WP:YEARLINK]] and [[:MOS:UNLINKYEARS]] using [[Project:AWB|AWB]] (8097)
revision.text:
[[File:鄭氏家廟正門.JPG|200px|thumb|The gate of Koxinga Ancestral Shrine]] [[Image:JapaneseKoxinga.JPG|right|thumb|200px|[[Tagawa Matsu|Koxinga's mother]] and him as a child]]
'''Koxinga Ancestral Shrine'''(鄭成功祖廟) is a family [[shrine]] built in [[Tainan City]], [[Taiwan]] in 1663 by [[Zheng Jing]], to worship his father [[Koxinga]].
When Taiwan became part of the [[Qing Dynasty|Qing Empire]], it was renamed "The Cheng's Ancestral Shrine" (鄭氏大宗祠) and today the official name is "Ancestral Shrine of Koxinga". The complex is traditional and elegant. There is an old well in front of the gate and this is all that remains of the original shrine.
The central hall worships the statue of Koxinga as well as the spirit tablets of each generation of ancestors. In 1771, there was a famous wooden tablet with the character "Three Generations Heritage" (三圭世錫) to prize the virtue of Koxinga's family.
In this shrine, there is also a sculpture of young Koxinga and [[Tagawa Matsu|his mother]].
==External links== * [http://tour.tncg.gov.tw/english/historic/ht01_1.asp?highspot_sn=534 Tainan City travel information] {{commonscat|Koxinga Ancestral Shrine}}
{{coord missing|Taiwan}}

[[zh:鄭成功祖廟]] [[ja:鄭成功祖廟]]

revision.text:
[[File:鄭氏家廟正門.JPG|200px|thumb|The gate of Koxinga Ancestral Shrine]] [[Image:JapaneseKoxinga.JPG|right|thumb|200px|[[Tagawa Matsu|Koxinga's mother]] and him as a child]]
'''Koxinga Ancestral Shrine''' ({{zh|c=鄭成功祖廟|p=Zhèngchénggōng Zǔmiào}}) is a family [[shrine]] built in [[West Central District]], [[Tainan]], [[Taiwan]] in 1663 by [[Zheng Jing]], to worship his father [[Koxinga]].
When Taiwan became part of the [[Qing Dynasty|Qing Empire]], it was renamed "The Cheng's Ancestral Shrine" (鄭氏大宗祠). During [[Taiwan under Japanese rule|Japanese rule]], the name was changed from 開山王廟 to Kaizan Shrine {{nihongo||開山神社}}. Today the official name is "Ancestral Shrine of Koxinga". The complex is traditional and elegant. There is an old well in front of the gate and this is all that remains of the original shrine.
The central hall worships the statue of Koxinga as well as the [[spirit tablets]] of each generation of ancestors. In 1771, there was a famous wooden tablet with the character "Three Generations Heritage" (三圭世錫) to prize the virtue of Koxinga's family.
In this shrine, there is also a sculpture of young Koxinga and his mother [[Tagawa Matsu]].
==See also== * [[List of tourist attractions in Taiwan]]
==External links== * [http://tour.tncg.gov.tw/english/historic/ht01_1.asp?highspot_sn=534 Tainan City travel information] {{commonscat|Koxinga Ancestral Shrine}}

When Taiwan became part of the [[Qing Dynasty|Qing Empire]], it was renamed "The Cheng's Ancestral Shrine" (鄭氏大宗祠) and today the official name is "Ancestral Shrine of Koxinga". The complex is traditional and elegant. There is an old well in front of the gate and this is all that remains of the original shrine.

In this shrine, there is also a sculpture of young Koxinga and his mother [[Tagawa Matsu]].

[[Image:JapaneseKoxinga.JPG|right|thumb|200px|[[Tagawa Matsu|Koxinga's mother]] and him as a child]]
'''Koxinga Ancestral Shrine''' ({{zh|c=鄭成功祖廟|p=Zhèngchénggōng Zǔmiào}}) is a family [[shrine]] built in [[West Central District]], [[Tainan]], [[Taiwan]] in 1663 by [[Zheng Jing]], to worship his father [[Koxinga]].
When Taiwan became [[Taiwan under Qing rule|part of]] the [[Qing dynasty]], it was renamed "The Cheng's Ancestral Shrine" (鄭氏大宗祠) and today the official name is "Ancestral Shrine of Koxinga". The complex is traditional and elegant. There is an old well in front of the gate and this is all that remains of the original shrine.
The central hall worships the statue of Koxinga as well as the [[spirit tablets]] of each generation of ancestors. In 1771, there was a famous wooden tablet with the character "Three Generations Heritage" (三圭世錫) to prize the virtue of Koxinga's family.
In this shrine, there is also a sculpture of young Koxinga and his mother [[Tagawa Matsu]].
It is not to be confused with the nearby [[Koxinga Shrine]]
==See also== * [[Chinese ancestral veneration]] * [[Beiji Temple]] * [[Grand Matsu Temple]] * [[Taiwan Confucian Temple]] * [[State Temple of the Martial God]] * [[Temple of the Five Concubines]] * [[List of temples in Taiwan]] * [[List of tourist attractions in Taiwan]]
==External links== {{commons category}} * [https://web.archive.org/web/20070927230240/http://tour.tncg.gov.tw/english/historic/ht01_1.asp?highspot_sn=534 Tainan City travel information]
[[Category:Koxinga]] [[Category:1663 establishments in Taiwan]] [[Category:Historic sites in Taiwan]] [[Category:Ancestral shrines in Taiwan]] [[Category:Religious buildings and structures in Tainan]]
{{Taiwan-religious-struct-stub}}

The central hall worships the statue of Koxinga as well as the [[spirit tablets]] of each generation of ancestors. In 1771, there was a famous wooden tablet with the character "Three Generations Heritage" (三圭世錫) to prize the virtue of Koxinga's family.
In this shrine, there is also a sculpture of young Koxinga and his mother [[Tagawa Matsu]].
It is not to be confused with the nearby [[Koxinga Shrine]]

[[Image:JapaneseKoxinga.JPG|right|thumb|200px|[[Tagawa Matsu|Koxinga's mother]] and him as a child]]
'''Koxinga Ancestral Shrine''' ({{zh|c=鄭成功祖廟|p=Zhèngchénggōng Zǔmiào}}) is a family [[shrine]] built in [[West Central District]], [[Tainan]], [[Taiwan]] in 1663 by [[Zheng Jing]], to worship his father [[Koxinga]].
When Taiwan became [[Taiwan under Qing rule|part of]] the [[Qing dynasty]], it was renamed "The Cheng's Ancestral Shrine" (鄭氏大宗祠) and today the official name is "Ancestral Shrine of Koxinga". The complex is traditional and elegant. There is an old well in front of the gate and this is all that remains of the original shrine.
The central hall worships the statue of Koxinga as well as the [[spirit tablets]] of each generation of ancestors. In 1771, there was a famous wooden tablet with the character "Three Generations Heritage" (三圭世錫) to prize the virtue of Koxinga's family.
In this shrine, there is also a sculpture of young Koxinga and his mother [[Tagawa Matsu]].
It is not to be confused with the nearby [[Koxinga Shrine]]
==See also== * [[Chinese ancestral veneration]] * [[Beiji Temple]] * [[Grand Matsu Temple]] * [[Taiwan Confucian Temple]] * [[State Temple of the Martial God]] * [[Temple of the Five Concubines]] * [[List of temples in Taiwan]] * [[List of tourist attractions in Taiwan]]
==External links== {{commons category}} * [https://web.archive.org/web/20070927230240/http://tour.tncg.gov.tw/english/historic/ht01_1.asp?highspot_sn=534 Tainan City travel information]

[[Image:JapaneseKoxinga.JPG|right|thumb|200px|[[Tagawa Matsu|Koxinga's mother]] and him as a child]]
'''Koxinga Ancestral Shrine''' ({{zh|c=鄭成功祖廟|p=Zhèngchénggōng Zǔmiào}}) is a family [[shrine]] built in [[West Central District]], [[Tainan]], [[Taiwan]] in 1663 by [[Zheng Jing]], to worship his father [[Koxinga]].
When Taiwan became [[Taiwan under Qing rule|part of]] the [[Qing dynasty]], it was renamed "The Cheng's Ancestral Shrine" (鄭氏大宗祠) and today the official name is "Ancestral Shrine of Koxinga". The complex is traditional and elegant. There is an old well in front of the gate and this is all that remains of the original shrine.
The central hall worships the statue of Koxinga as well as the [[spirit tablets]] of each generation of ancestors. In 1771, there was a famous wooden tablet with the character "Three Generations Heritage" (三圭世錫) to prize the virtue of Koxinga's family.
In this shrine, there is also a sculpture of young Koxinga and his mother [[Tagawa Matsu]].
It is not to be confused with the nearby [[Koxinga Shrine]]
==See also== * [[Chinese ancestral veneration]] * [[Beiji Temple]] * [[Grand Matsu Temple]] * [[Taiwan Confucian Temple]] * [[State Temple of the Martial God]] * [[Temple of the Five Concubines]] * [[List of temples in Taiwan]] * [[List of tourist attractions in Taiwan]]

[[Image:JapaneseKoxinga.JPG|right|thumb|200px|[[Tagawa Matsu|Koxinga's mother]] and him as a child]]
'''Koxinga Ancestral Shrine''' ({{zh|c=鄭成功祖廟|p=Zhèngchénggōng Zǔmiào}}) is a family [[shrine]] built in [[West Central District]], [[Tainan]], [[Taiwan]] in 1663 by [[Zheng Jing]], to worship his father [[Koxinga]].
When Taiwan became [[Taiwan under Qing rule|part of]] the [[Qing dynasty]], it was renamed "The Cheng's Ancestral Shrine" (鄭氏大宗祠) and today the official name is "Ancestral Shrine of Koxinga". The complex is traditional and elegant. There is an old well in front of the gate and this is all that remains of the original shrine.
The central hall worships the statue of Koxinga as well as the [[spirit tablets]] of each generation of ancestors. In 1771, there was a famous wooden tablet with the character "Three Generations Heritage" (三圭世錫) to prize the virtue of Koxinga's family.
In this shrine, there is also a sculpture of young Koxinga and his mother [[Tagawa Matsu]].
It is not to be confused with the nearby [[Koxinga Shrine]]
==See also== * [[Chinese ancestral veneration]] * [[Beiji Temple]] * [[Grand Matsu Temple]] * [[Taiwan Confucian Temple]] * [[State Temple of the Martial God]] * [[Temple of the Five Concubines]] * [[List of temples in Taiwan]] * [[List of tourist attractions in Taiwan]]

'''Koxinga Ancestral Shrine''' ({{zh|c=鄭成功祖廟|p=Zhèngchénggōng Zǔmiào}}) is a family [[shrine]] built in [[West Central District]], [[Tainan]], [[Taiwan]] in 1663 by [[Zheng Jing]], to worship his father [[Koxinga]].
When Taiwan became [[Taiwan under Qing rule|part of]] the [[Qing dynasty]], it was renamed "The Cheng's Ancestral Shrine" (鄭氏大宗祠) and today the official name is "Ancestral Shrine of Koxinga". The complex is traditional and elegant. There is an old well in front of the gate and this is all that remains of the original shrine.
The central hall worships the statue of Koxinga as well as the [[spirit tablets]] of each generation of ancestors. In 1771, there was a famous wooden tablet with the character "Three Generations Heritage" (三圭世錫) to prize the virtue of Koxinga's family.
In this shrine, there is also a sculpture of young Koxinga and his mother [[Tagawa Matsu]].
It is not to be confused with the nearby [[Koxinga Shrine]]

[[Image:JapaneseKoxinga.JPG|right|thumb|200px|[[Tagawa Matsu|Koxinga's mother]] and him as a child]]
'''Koxinga Ancestral Shrine''' ({{zh|c=鄭成功祖廟|p=Zhèngchénggōng Zǔmiào}}) is a family [[shrine]] built in [[West Central District]], [[Tainan]], [[Taiwan]] in 1663 by [[Zheng Jing]], to worship his father [[Koxinga]].
When Taiwan became [[Taiwan under Qing rule|part of]] the [[Qing dynasty]], it was renamed "The Cheng's Ancestral Shrine" (鄭氏大宗祠) and today the official name is "Ancestral Shrine of Koxinga". The complex is traditional and elegant. There is an old well in front of the gate and this is all that remains of the original shrine.
The central hall worships the statue of Koxinga as well as the [[spirit tablets]] of each generation of ancestors. In 1771, there was a famous wooden tablet with the character "Three Generations Heritage" (三圭世錫) to prize the virtue of Koxinga's family.
In this shrine, there is also a sculpture of young Koxinga and his mother [[Tagawa Matsu]].
It is not to be confused with the nearby [[Koxinga Shrine]]
==See also== * [[Chinese ancestral veneration]] * [[Beiji Temple]] * [[Grand Matsu Temple]] * [[Taiwan Confucian Temple]] * [[State Temple of the Martial God]] * [[Temple of the Five Concubines]] * [[List of temples in Taiwan]] * [[List of tourist attractions in Taiwan]]

'''Koxinga Ancestral Shrine''' ({{zh|c=鄭成功祖廟|p=Zhèngchénggōng Zǔmiào}}) is a family [[shrine]] built in [[West Central District]], [[Tainan]], [[Taiwan]] in 1663 by [[Zheng Jing]], to worship his father [[Koxinga]].
When Taiwan became [[Taiwan under Qing rule|part of]] the [[Qing dynasty]], it was renamed "The Cheng's Ancestral Shrine" (鄭氏大宗祠) and today the official name is "Ancestral Shrine of Koxinga". The complex is traditional and elegant. There is an old well in front of the gate and this is all that remains of the original shrine.
The central hall worships the statue of Koxinga as well as the [[spirit tablets]] of each generation of ancestors. In 1771, there was a famous wooden tablet with the character "Three Generations Heritage" (三圭世錫) to prize the virtue of Koxinga's family.
In this shrine, there is also a sculpture of young Koxinga and his mother [[Tagawa Matsu]].
It is not to be confused with the nearby [[Koxinga Shrine]]

'''Koxinga Ancestral Shrine''' ({{zh|c=鄭成功祖廟|p=Zhèngchénggōng Zǔmiào}}) is a family [[shrine]] built in [[West Central District]], [[Tainan]], [[Taiwan]] in 1663 by [[Zheng Jing]], to worship his father [[Koxinga]].
When Taiwan became [[Taiwan under Qing rule|part of]] the [[Qing dynasty]], it was renamed "The Cheng's Ancestral Shrine" (鄭氏大宗祠) and today the official name is "Ancestral Shrine of Koxinga". The complex is traditional and elegant. There is an old well in front of the gate and this is all that remains of the original shrine.
The central hall worships the statue of Koxinga as well as the [[spirit tablets]] of each generation of ancestors. In 1771, there was a famous wooden tablet with the character "Three Generations Heritage" (三圭世錫) to prize the virtue of Koxinga's family.
In this shrine, there is also a sculpture of young Koxinga and his mother [[Tagawa Matsu]].
It is not to be confused with the nearby [[Koxinga Shrine]]
==See also== * [[Chinese ancestral veneration]] * [[Beiji Temple]] * [[Grand Matsu Temple]] * [[Taiwan Confucian Temple]] * [[State Temple of the Martial God]] * [[Temple of the Five Concubines]] * [[List of temples in Taiwan]] * [[List of tourist attractions in Taiwan]]

When Taiwan became [[Taiwan under Qing rule|part of]] the [[Qing dynasty]], it was renamed "The Cheng's Ancestral Shrine" (鄭氏大宗祠) and today the official name is "Ancestral Shrine of Koxinga". [[Image:JapaneseKoxinga.JPG|right|thumb|200px|[[Tagawa Matsu|Koxinga's mother]] and him as a child]]
'''Koxinga Ancestral Shrine''' ({{zh|c=鄭成功祖廟|p=Zhèngchénggōng Zǔmiào}}) is a family [[shrine]] built in [[West Central District]], [[Tainan]], [[Taiwan]] in 1663 by [[Zheng Jing]], to worship his father [[Koxinga]].
When Taiwan became [[Taiwan under Qing rule|part of]] the [[Qing dynasty]], it was renamed "The Cheng's Ancestral Shrine" (鄭氏大宗祠) and today the official name is "Ancestral Shrine of Koxinga". The complex is traditional and elegant. There is an old well in front of the gate and this is all that remains of the original shrine.
The central hall worships the statue of Koxinga as well as the [[spirit tablets]] of each generation of ancestors. In 1771, there was a famous wooden tablet with the character "Three Generations Heritage" (三圭世錫) to prize the virtue of Koxinga's family.
In this shrine, there is also a sculpture of young Koxinga and his mother [[Tagawa Matsu]].
It is not to be confused with the nearby [[Koxinga Shrine]]
==See also== * [[Chinese ancestral veneration]] * [[Beiji Temple]] * [[Grand Matsu Temple]] * [[Taiwan Confucian Temple]] * [[State Temple of the Martial God]] * [[Temple of the Five Concubines]] * [[List of temples in Taiwan]] * [[List of tourist attractions in Taiwan]]

[[Image:JapaneseKoxinga.JPG|right|thumb|200px|[[Tagawa Matsu|Koxinga's mother]] and him as a child]]
'''Koxinga Ancestral Shrine''' ({{zh|c=鄭成功祖廟|p=Zhèngchénggōng Zǔmiào}}) is a family [[shrine]] built in [[West Central District]], [[Tainan]], [[Taiwan]] in 1663 by [[Zheng Jing]], to worship his father [[Koxinga]].
When Taiwan became [[Taiwan under Qing rule|part of]] the [[Qing dynasty]], it was renamed "The Cheng's Ancestral Shrine" (鄭氏大宗祠) and today the official name is "Ancestral Shrine of Koxinga". The complex is traditional and elegant. There is an old well in front of the gate and this is all that remains of the original shrine.
The central hall worships the statue of Koxinga as well as the [[spirit tablets]] of each generation of ancestors. In 1771, there was a famous wooden tablet with the character "Three Generations Heritage" (三圭世錫) to prize the virtue of Koxinga's family.
In this shrine, there is also a sculpture of young Koxinga and his mother [[Tagawa Matsu]].
It is not to be confused with the nearby [[Koxinga Shrine]]
==See also== * [[Chinese ancestral veneration]] * [[Beiji Temple]] * [[Grand Matsu Temple]] * [[Taiwan Confucian Temple]] * [[State Temple of the Martial God]] * [[Temple of the Five Concubines]] * [[List of temples in Taiwan]] * [[List of tourist attractions in Taiwan]]

'''Koxinga Ancestral Shrine''' ({{zh|c=鄭成功祖廟|p=Zhèngchénggōng Zǔmiào}}) is a family [[shrine]] built in [[West Central District]], [[Tainan]], [[Taiwan]] in 1663 by [[Zheng Jing]], to worship his father [[Koxinga]].
When Taiwan became [[Taiwan under Qing rule|part of]] the [[Qing dynasty]], it was renamed "The Cheng's Ancestral Shrine" (鄭氏大宗祠) and today the official name is "Ancestral Shrine of Koxinga". The complex is traditional and elegant. There is an old well in front of the gate and this is all that remains of the original shrine.
The central hall worships the statue of Koxinga as well as the [[spirit tablets]] of each generation of ancestors. In 1771, there was a famous wooden tablet with the character "Three Generations Heritage" (三圭世錫) to prize the virtue of Koxinga's family.
In this shrine, there is also a sculpture of young Koxinga and his mother [[Tagawa Matsu]].
It is not to be confused with the nearby [[Koxinga Shrine]]

[[Image:JapaneseKoxinga.JPG|right|thumb|200px|[[Tagawa Matsu|Koxinga's mother]] and him as a child]]
'''Koxinga Ancestral Shrine''' ({{zh|c=鄭成功祖廟|p=Zhèngchénggōng Zǔmiào}}) is a family [[shrine]] built in [[West Central District]], [[Tainan]], [[Taiwan]] in 1663 by [[Zheng Jing]], to worship his father [[Koxinga]].
When Taiwan became [[Taiwan under Qing rule|part of]] the [[Qing dynasty]], it was renamed "The Cheng's Ancestral Shrine" (鄭氏大宗祠) and today the official name is "Ancestral Shrine of Koxinga". The complex is traditional and elegant. There is an old well in front of the gate and this is all that remains of the original shrine.
The central hall worships the statue of Koxinga as well as the [[spirit tablets]] of each generation of ancestors. In 1771, there was a famous wooden tablet with the character "Three Generations Heritage" (三圭世錫) to prize the virtue of Koxinga's family.
In this shrine, there is also a sculpture of young Koxinga and his mother [[Tagawa Matsu]].
It is not to be confused with the nearby [[Koxinga Shrine]]
==See also== * [[Chinese ancestral veneration]] * [[Beiji Temple]] * [[Grand Matsu Temple]] * [[Taiwan Confucian Temple]] * [[State Temple of the Martial God]] * [[Temple of the Five Concubines]] * [[List of temples in Taiwan]] * [[List of tourist attractions in Taiwan]]

When Taiwan became [[Taiwan under Qing rule|part of]] the [[Qing dynasty]], it was renamed "The Cheng's Ancestral Shrine" (鄭氏大宗祠) and today the official name is "Ancestral Shrine of Koxinga". The complex is traditional and elegant. There is an old well in front of the gate and this is all that remains of the original shrine.
The central hall worships the statue of Koxinga as well as the [[spirit tablets]] of each generation of ancestors. In 1771, there was a famous wooden tablet with the character "Three Generations Heritage" (三圭世錫) to prize the virtue of Koxinga's family.
In this shrine, there is also a sculpture of young Koxinga and his mother [[Tagawa Matsu]].
It is not to be confused with the nearby [[Koxinga Shrine]]

When Taiwan became [[Taiwan under Qing rule|part of]] the [[Qing dynasty]], it was renamed "The Cheng's Ancestral Shrine" (鄭氏大宗祠) and today the official name is "Ancestral Shrine of Koxinga". The complex is traditional and elegant. There is an old well in front of the gate and this is all that remains of the original shrine.
The central hall worships the statue of Koxinga as well as the [[spirit tablets]] of each generation of ancestors. In 1771, there was a famous wooden tablet with the character "Three Generations Heritage" (三圭世錫) to prize the virtue of Koxinga's family.
In this shrine, there is also a sculpture of young Koxinga and his mother [[Tagawa Matsu]].
It is not to be confused with the nearby [[Koxinga Shrine]]

[[Image:JapaneseKoxinga.JPG|right|thumb|200px|[[Tagawa Matsu|Koxinga's mother]] and him as a child]]
'''Koxinga Ancestral Shrine''' ({{zh|c=鄭成功祖廟|p=Zhèngchénggōng Zǔmiào}}) is a family [[shrine]] built in [[West Central District]], [[Tainan]], [[Taiwan]] in 1663 by [[Zheng Jing]], to worship his father [[Koxinga]].
When Taiwan became [[Taiwan under Qing rule|part of]] the [[Qing dynasty]], it was renamed "The Cheng's Ancestral Shrine" (鄭氏大宗祠) and today the official name is "Ancestral Shrine of Koxinga". The complex is traditional and elegant. There is an old well in front of the gate and this is all that remains of the original shrine.
The central hall worships the statue of Koxinga as well as the [[spirit tablets]] of each generation of ancestors. In 1771, there was a famous wooden tablet with the character "Three Generations Heritage" (三圭世錫) to prize the virtue of Koxinga's family.
In this shrine, there is also a sculpture of young Koxinga and his mother [[Tagawa Matsu]].
It is not to be confused with the nearby [[Koxinga Shrine]]
==See also== * [[Chinese ancestral veneration]] * [[Beiji Temple]] * [[Grand Matsu Temple]] * [[Taiwan Confucian Temple]] * [[State Temple of the Martial God]] * [[Temple of the Five Concubines]] * [[List of temples in Taiwan]] * [[List of tourist attractions in Taiwan]]

[[Image:JapaneseKoxinga.JPG|right|thumb|200px|[[Tagawa Matsu|Koxinga's mother]] and him as a child]]
'''Koxinga Ancestral Shrine''' ({{zh|c=鄭成功祖廟|p=Zhèngchénggōng Zǔmiào}}) is a family [[shrine]] built in [[West Central District]], [[Tainan]], [[Taiwan]] in 1663 by [[Zheng Jing]], to worship his father [[Koxinga]].
When Taiwan became [[Taiwan under Qing rule|part of]] the [[Qing dynasty]], it was renamed "The Cheng's Ancestral Shrine" (鄭氏大宗祠) and today the official name is "Ancestral Shrine of Koxinga". The complex is traditional and elegant. There is an old well in front of the gate and this is all that remains of the original shrine.
The central hall worships the statue of Koxinga as well as the [[spirit tablets]] of each generation of ancestors. In 1771, there was a famous wooden tablet with the character "Three Generations Heritage" (三圭世錫) to prize the virtue of Koxinga's family.
In this shrine, there is also a sculpture of young Koxinga and his mother [[Tagawa Matsu]].
It is not to be confused with the nearby [[Koxinga Shrine]]
==See also== * [[Chinese ancestral veneration]] * [[Beiji Temple]] * [[Grand Matsu Temple]] * [[Taiwan Confucian Temple]] * [[State Temple of the Martial God]] * [[Temple of the Five Concubines]] * [[List of temples in Taiwan]] * [[List of tourist attractions in Taiwan]]

[[Image:JapaneseKoxinga.JPG|right|thumb|200px|[[Tagawa Matsu|Koxinga's mother]] and him as a child]]
'''Koxinga Ancestral Shrine''' ({{zh|c=鄭成功祖廟|p=Zhèngchénggōng Zǔmiào}}) is a family [[shrine]] built in [[West Central District]], [[Tainan]], [[Taiwan]] in 1663 by [[Zheng Jing]], to worship his father [[Koxinga]].
When Taiwan became [[Taiwan under Qing rule|part of]] the [[Qing dynasty]], it was renamed "The Cheng's Ancestral Shrine" (鄭氏大宗祠) and today the official name is "Ancestral Shrine of Koxinga". The complex is traditional and elegant. There is an old well in front of the gate and this is all that remains of the original shrine.
The central hall worships the statue of Koxinga as well as the [[spirit tablets]] of each generation of ancestors. In 1771, there was a famous wooden tablet with the character "Three Generations Heritage" (三圭世錫) to prize the virtue of Koxinga's family.
In this shrine, there is also a sculpture of young Koxinga and his mother [[Tagawa Matsu]].
It is not to be confused with the nearby [[Koxinga Shrine]]
==See also== * [[Chinese ancestral veneration]] * [[Beiji Temple]] * [[Grand Matsu Temple]] * [[Taiwan Confucian Temple]] * [[State Temple of the Martial God]] * [[Temple of the Five Concubines]] * [[List of temples in Taiwan]] * [[List of tourist attractions in Taiwan]]

[[Image:JapaneseKoxinga.JPG|right|thumb|200px|[[Tagawa Matsu|Koxinga's mother]] and him as a child]]
'''Koxinga Ancestral Shrine''' ({{zh|c=鄭成功祖廟|p=Zhèngchénggōng Zǔmiào}}) is a family [[shrine]] built in [[West Central District]], [[Tainan]], [[Taiwan]] in 1663 by [[Zheng Jing]], to worship his father [[Koxinga]].
When Taiwan became [[Taiwan under Qing rule|part of]] the [[Qing dynasty]], it was renamed "The Cheng's Ancestral Shrine" (鄭氏大宗祠) and today the official name is "Ancestral Shrine of Koxinga". The complex is traditional and elegant. There is an old well in front of the gate and this is all that remains of the original shrine.
The central hall worships the statue of Koxinga as well as the [[spirit tablets]] of each generation of ancestors. In 1771, there was a famous wooden tablet with the character "Three Generations Heritage" (三圭世錫) to prize the virtue of Koxinga's family.
In this shrine, there is also a sculpture of young Koxinga and his mother [[Tagawa Matsu]].
It is not to be confused with the nearby [[Koxinga Shrine]]
==See also== * [[Chinese ancestral veneration]] * [[Beiji Temple]] * [[Grand Matsu Temple]] * [[Taiwan Confucian Temple]] * [[State Temple of the Martial God]] * [[Temple of the Five Concubines]] * [[List of temples in Taiwan]] * [[List of tourist attractions in Taiwan]]

[[Image:JapaneseKoxinga.JPG|right|thumb|200px|[[Tagawa Matsu|Koxinga's mother]] and him as a child]]
'''Koxinga Ancestral Shrine''' ({{zh|c=鄭成功祖廟|p=Zhèngchénggōng Zǔmiào}}) is a family [[shrine]] built in [[West Central District]], [[Tainan]], [[Taiwan]] in 1663 by [[Zheng Jing]], to worship his father [[Koxinga]].
When Taiwan became [[Taiwan under Qing rule|part of]] the [[Qing dynasty]], it was renamed "The Cheng's Ancestral Shrine" (鄭氏大宗祠) and today the official name is "Ancestral Shrine of Koxinga". The complex is traditional and elegant. There is an old well in front of the gate and this is all that remains of the original shrine.
The central hall worships the statue of Koxinga as well as the [[spirit tablets]] of each generation of ancestors. In 1771, there was a famous wooden tablet with the character "Three Generations Heritage" (三圭世錫) to prize the virtue of Koxinga's family.
In this shrine, there is also a sculpture of young Koxinga and his mother [[Tagawa Matsu]].
It is not to be confused with the nearby [[Koxinga Shrine]]
==See also== * [[Chinese ancestral veneration]] * [[Beiji Temple]] * [[Grand Matsu Temple]] * [[Taiwan Confucian Temple]] * [[State Temple of the Martial God]] * [[Temple of the Five Concubines]] * [[List of temples in Taiwan]] * [[List of tourist attractions in Taiwan]]

[[Image:JapaneseKoxinga.JPG|right|thumb|200px|[[Tagawa Matsu|Koxinga's mother]] and him as a child]]
'''Koxinga Ancestral Shrine''' ({{zh|c=鄭成功祖廟|p=Zhèngchénggōng Zǔmiào}}) is a family [[shrine]] built in [[West Central District]], [[Tainan]], [[Taiwan]] in 1663 by [[Zheng Jing]], to worship his father [[Koxinga]].
When Taiwan became [[Taiwan under Qing rule|part of]] the [[Qing dynasty]], it was renamed "The Cheng's Ancestral Shrine" (鄭氏大宗祠) and today the official name is "Ancestral Shrine of Koxinga". The complex is traditional and elegant. There is an old well in front of the gate and this is all that remains of the original shrine.
The central hall worships the statue of Koxinga as well as the [[spirit tablets]] of each generation of ancestors. In 1771, there was a famous wooden tablet with the character "Three Generations Heritage" (三圭世錫) to prize the virtue of Koxinga's family.
In this shrine, there is also a sculpture of young Koxinga and his mother [[Tagawa Matsu]].
It is not to be confused with the nearby [[Koxinga Shrine]]
==See also== * [[Chinese ancestral veneration]] * [[Beiji Temple]] * [[Grand Matsu Temple]] * [[Taiwan Confucian Temple]] * [[State Temple of the Martial God]] * [[Temple of the Five Concubines]] * [[List of temples in Taiwan]] * [[List of tourist attractions in Taiwan]]

[[Image:JapaneseKoxinga.JPG|right|thumb|200px|[[Tagawa Matsu|Koxinga's mother]] and him as a child]]
'''Koxinga Ancestral Shrine''' ({{zh|c=鄭成功祖廟|p=Zhèngchénggōng Zǔmiào}}) is a family [[shrine]] built in [[West Central District]], [[Tainan]], [[Taiwan]] in 1663 by [[Zheng Jing]], to worship his father [[Koxinga]].
When Taiwan became [[Taiwan under Qing rule|part of]] the [[Qing dynasty]], it was renamed "The Cheng's Ancestral Shrine" (鄭氏大宗祠) and today the official name is "Ancestral Shrine of Koxinga". The complex is traditional and elegant. There is an old well in front of the gate and this is all that remains of the original shrine.
The central hall worships the statue of Koxinga as well as the [[spirit tablets]] of each generation of ancestors. In 1771, there was a famous wooden tablet with the character "Three Generations Heritage" (三圭世錫) to prize the virtue of Koxinga's family.
In this shrine, there is also a sculpture of young Koxinga and his mother [[Tagawa Matsu]].
It is not to be confused with the nearby [[Koxinga Shrine]]
==See also== * [[Chinese ancestral veneration]] * [[Beiji Temple]] * [[Grand Matsu Temple]] * [[Taiwan Confucian Temple]] * [[State Temple of the Martial God]] * [[Temple of the Five Concubines]] * [[List of temples in Taiwan]] * [[List of tourist attractions in Taiwan]]

[[Image:JapaneseKoxinga.JPG|right|thumb|200px|[[Tagawa Matsu|Koxinga's mother]] and him as a child]]
'''Koxinga Ancestral Shrine''' ({{zh|c=鄭成功祖廟|p=Zhèngchénggōng Zǔmiào}}) is a family [[shrine]] built in [[West Central District]], [[Tainan]], [[Taiwan]] in 1663 by [[Zheng Jing]], to worship his father [[Koxinga]].
When Taiwan became [[Taiwan under Qing rule|part of]] the [[Qing dynasty]], it was renamed "The Cheng's Ancestral Shrine" (鄭氏大宗祠) and today the official name is "Ancestral Shrine of Koxinga". The complex is traditional and elegant. There is an old well in front of the gate and this is all that remains of the original shrine.
The central hall worships the statue of Koxinga as well as the [[spirit tablets]] of each generation of ancestors. In 1771, there was a famous wooden tablet with the character "Three Generations Heritage" (三圭世錫) to prize the virtue of Koxinga's family.
In this shrine, there is also a sculpture of young Koxinga and his mother [[Tagawa Matsu]].
It is not to be confused with the nearby [[Koxinga Shrine]]
==See also== * [[Chinese ancestral veneration]] * [[Beiji Temple]] * [[Grand Matsu Temple]] * [[Taiwan Confucian Temple]] * [[State Temple of the Martial God]] * [[Temple of the Five Concubines]] * [[List of temples in Taiwan]] * [[List of tourist attractions in Taiwan]]

[[Image:JapaneseKoxinga.JPG|right|thumb|200px|[[Tagawa Matsu|Koxinga's mother]] and him as a child]]
'''Koxinga Ancestral Shrine''' ({{zh|c=鄭成功祖廟|p=Zhèngchénggōng Zǔmiào}}) is a family [[shrine]] built in [[West Central District]], [[Tainan]], [[Taiwan]] in 1663 by [[Zheng Jing]], to worship his father [[Koxinga]].
When Taiwan became [[Taiwan under Qing rule|part of]] the [[Qing dynasty]], it was renamed "The Cheng's Ancestral Shrine" (鄭氏大宗祠) and today the official name is "Ancestral Shrine of Koxinga". The complex is traditional and elegant. There is an old well in front of the gate and this is all that remains of the original shrine.
The central hall worships the statue of Koxinga as well as the [[spirit tablets]] of each generation of ancestors. In 1771, there was a famous wooden tablet with the character "Three Generations Heritage" (三圭世錫) to prize the virtue of Koxinga's family.
In this shrine, there is also a sculpture of young Koxinga and his mother [[Tagawa Matsu]].
It is not to be confused with the nearby [[Koxinga Shrine]]
==See also== * [[Chinese ancestral veneration]] * [[Beiji Temple]] * [[Grand Matsu Temple]] * [[Taiwan Confucian Temple]] * [[State Temple of the Martial God]] * [[Temple of the Five Concubines]] * [[List of temples in Taiwan]] * [[List of tourist attractions in Taiwan]]

[[Image:JapaneseKoxinga.JPG|right|thumb|200px|[[Tagawa Matsu|Koxinga's mother]] and him as a child]]
'''Koxinga Ancestral Shrine''' ({{zh|c=鄭成功祖廟|p=Zhèngchénggōng Zǔmiào}}) is a family [[shrine]] built in [[West Central District]], [[Tainan]], [[Taiwan]] in 1663 by [[Zheng Jing]], to worship his father [[Koxinga]].
When Taiwan became [[Taiwan under Qing rule|part of]] the [[Qing dynasty]], it was renamed "The Cheng's Ancestral Shrine" (鄭氏大宗祠) and today the official name is "Ancestral Shrine of Koxinga". The complex is traditional and elegant. There is an old well in front of the gate and this is all that remains of the original shrine.
The central hall worships the statue of Koxinga as well as the [[spirit tablets]] of each generation of ancestors. In 1771, there was a famous wooden tablet with the character "Three Generations Heritage" (三圭世錫) to prize the virtue of Koxinga's family.
In this shrine, there is also a sculpture of young Koxinga and his mother [[Tagawa Matsu]].
It is not to be confused with the nearby [[Koxinga Shrine]]
==See also== * [[Chinese ancestral veneration]] * [[Beiji Temple]] * [[Grand Matsu Temple]] * [[Taiwan Confucian Temple]] * [[State Temple of the Martial God]] * [[Temple of the Five Concubines]] * [[List of temples in Taiwan]] * [[List of tourist attractions in Taiwan]]

[[Image:JapaneseKoxinga.JPG|right|thumb|200px|[[Tagawa Matsu|Koxinga's mother]] and him as a child]]
'''Koxinga Ancestral Shrine''' ({{zh|c=鄭成功祖廟|p=Zhèngchénggōng Zǔmiào}}) is a family [[shrine]] built in [[West Central District]], [[Tainan]], [[Taiwan]] in 1663 by [[Zheng Jing]], to worship his father [[Koxinga]].
When Taiwan became [[Taiwan under Qing rule|part of]] the [[Qing dynasty]], it was renamed "The Cheng's Ancestral Shrine" (鄭氏大宗祠) and today the official name is "Ancestral Shrine of Koxinga". The complex is traditional and elegant. There is an old well in front of the gate and this is all that remains of the original shrine.
The central hall worships the statue of Koxinga as well as the [[spirit tablets]] of each generation of ancestors. In 1771, there was a famous wooden tablet with the character "Three Generations Heritage" (三圭世錫) to prize the virtue of Koxinga's family.
In this shrine, there is also a sculpture of young Koxinga and his mother [[Tagawa Matsu]].
It is not to be confused with the nearby [[Koxinga Shrine]]
==See also== * [[Chinese ancestral veneration]] * [[Beiji Temple]] * [[Grand Matsu Temple]] * [[Taiwan Confucian Temple]] * [[State Temple of the Martial God]] * [[Temple of the Five Concubines]] * [[List of temples in Taiwan]] * [[List of tourist attractions in Taiwan]]

[[Image:JapaneseKoxinga.JPG|right|thumb|200px|[[Tagawa Matsu|Koxinga's mother]] and him as a child]]
'''Koxinga Ancestral Shrine''' ({{zh|c=鄭成功祖廟|p=Zhèngchénggōng Zǔmiào}}) is a family [[shrine]] built in [[West Central District]], [[Tainan]], [[Taiwan]] in 1663 by [[Zheng Jing]], to worship his father [[Koxinga]].
When Taiwan became [[Taiwan under Qing rule|part of]] the [[Qing dynasty]], it was renamed "The Cheng's Ancestral Shrine" (鄭氏大宗祠) and today the official name is "Ancestral Shrine of Koxinga". The complex is traditional and elegant. There is an old well in front of the gate and this is all that remains of the original shrine.
The central hall worships the statue of Koxinga as well as the [[spirit tablets]] of each generation of ancestors. In 1771, there was a famous wooden tablet with the character "Three Generations Heritage" (三圭世錫) to prize the virtue of Koxinga's family.
In this shrine, there is also a sculpture of young Koxinga and his mother [[Tagawa Matsu]].
It is not to be confused with the nearby [[Koxinga Shrine]]
==See also== * [[Chinese ancestral veneration]] * [[Beiji Temple]] * [[Grand Matsu Temple]] * [[Taiwan Confucian Temple]] * [[State Temple of the Martial God]] * [[Temple of the Five Concubines]] * [[List of temples in Taiwan]] * [[List of tourist attractions in Taiwan]]

[[Image:JapaneseKoxinga.JPG|right|thumb|200px|[[Tagawa Matsu|Koxinga's mother]] and him as a child]]
'''Koxinga Ancestral Shrine''' ({{zh|c=鄭成功祖廟|p=Zhèngchénggōng Zǔmiào}}) is a family [[shrine]] built in [[West Central District]], [[Tainan]], [[Taiwan]] in 1663 by [[Zheng Jing]], to worship his father [[Koxinga]].
When Taiwan became [[Taiwan under Qing rule|part of]] the [[Qing dynasty]], it was renamed "The Cheng's Ancestral Shrine" (鄭氏大宗祠) and today the official name is "Ancestral Shrine of Koxinga". The complex is traditional and elegant. There is an old well in front of the gate and this is all that remains of the original shrine.
The central hall worships the statue of Koxinga as well as the [[spirit tablets]] of each generation of ancestors. In 1771, there was a famous wooden tablet with the character "Three Generations Heritage" (三圭世錫) to prize the virtue of Koxinga's family.
In this shrine, there is also a sculpture of young Koxinga and his mother [[Tagawa Matsu]].
It is not to be confused with the nearby [[Koxinga Shrine]]
==See also== * [[Chinese ancestral veneration]] * [[Beiji Temple]] * [[Grand Matsu Temple]] * [[Taiwan Confucian Temple]] * [[State Temple of the Martial God]] * [[Temple of the Five Concubines]] * [[List of temples in Taiwan]] * [[List of tourist attractions in Taiwan]]

[[Image:JapaneseKoxinga.JPG|right|thumb|200px|[[Tagawa Matsu|Koxinga's mother]] and him as a child]]
'''Koxinga Ancestral Shrine''' ({{zh|c=鄭成功祖廟|p=Zhèngchénggōng Zǔmiào}}) is a family [[shrine]] built in [[West Central District]], [[Tainan]], [[Taiwan]] in 1663 by [[Zheng Jing]], to worship his father [[Koxinga]].
When Taiwan became [[Taiwan under Qing rule|part of]] the [[Qing dynasty]], it was renamed "The Cheng's Ancestral Shrine" (鄭氏大宗祠) and today the official name is "Ancestral Shrine of Koxinga". The complex is traditional and elegant. There is an old well in front of the gate and this is all that remains of the original shrine.
The central hall worships the statue of Koxinga as well as the [[spirit tablets]] of each generation of ancestors. In 1771, there was a famous wooden tablet with the character "Three Generations Heritage" (三圭世錫) to prize the virtue of Koxinga's family.
In this shrine, there is also a sculpture of young Koxinga and his mother [[Tagawa Matsu]].
It is not to be confused with the nearby [[Koxinga Shrine]]
==See also== * [[Chinese ancestral veneration]] * [[Beiji Temple]] * [[Grand Matsu Temple]] * [[Taiwan Confucian Temple]] * [[State Temple of the Martial God]] * [[Temple of the Five Concubines]] * [[List of temples in Taiwan]] * [[List of tourist attractions in Taiwan]]

[[Image:JapaneseKoxinga.JPG|right|thumb|200px|[[Tagawa Matsu|Koxinga's mother]] and him as a child]]
'''Koxinga Ancestral Shrine''' ({{zh|c=鄭成功祖廟|p=Zhèngchénggōng Zǔmiào}}) is a family [[shrine]] built in [[West Central District]], [[Tainan]], [[Taiwan]] in 1663 by [[Zheng Jing]], to worship his father [[Koxinga]].
When Taiwan became [[Taiwan under Qing rule|part of]] the [[Qing dynasty]], it was renamed "The Cheng's Ancestral Shrine" (鄭氏大宗祠) and today the official name is "Ancestral Shrine of Koxinga". The complex is traditional and elegant. There is an old well in front of the gate and this is all that remains of the original shrine.
The central hall worships the statue of Koxinga as well as the [[spirit tablets]] of each generation of ancestors. In 1771, there was a famous wooden tablet with the character "Three Generations Heritage" (三圭世錫) to prize the virtue of Koxinga's family.
In this shrine, there is also a sculpture of young Koxinga and his mother [[Tagawa Matsu]].
It is not to be confused with the nearby [[Koxinga Shrine]]
==See also== * [[Chinese ancestral veneration]] * [[Beiji Temple]] * [[Grand Matsu Temple]] * [[Taiwan Confucian Temple]] * [[State Temple of the Martial God]] * [[Temple of the Five Concubines]] * [[List of temples in Taiwan]] * [[List of tourist attractions in Taiwan]]

When Taiwan became [[Taiwan under Qing rule|part of]] the [[Qing dynasty]], it was renamed "The Cheng's Ancestral Shrine" (鄭氏大宗祠) and today the official name is "Ancestral Shrine of Koxinga". The complex is traditional and elegant. There is an old well in front of the gate and this is all that remains of the original shrine.
The central hall worships the statue of Koxinga as well as the [[spirit tablets]] of each generation of ancestors. In 1771, there was a famous wooden tablet with the character "Three Generations Heritage" (三圭世錫) to prize the virtue of Koxinga's family.

[[Image:JapaneseKoxinga.JPG|right|thumb|200px|[[Tagawa Matsu|Koxinga's mother]] and him as a child]]
'''Koxinga Ancestral Shrine''' ({{zh|c=鄭成功祖廟|p=Zhèngchénggōng Zǔmiào}}) is a family [[shrine]] built in [[West Central District]], [[Tainan]], [[Taiwan]] in 1663 by [[Zheng Jing]], to worship his father [[Koxinga]].
When Taiwan became [[Taiwan under Qing rule|part of]] the [[Qing dynasty]], it was renamed "The Cheng's Ancestral Shrine" (鄭氏大宗祠) and today the official name is "Ancestral Shrine of Koxinga". The complex is traditional and elegant. There is an old well in front of the gate and this is all that remains of the original shrine.
The central hall worships the statue of Koxinga as well as the [[spirit tablets]] of each generation of ancestors. In 1771, there was a famous wooden tablet with the character "Three Generations Heritage" (三圭世錫) to prize the virtue of Koxinga's family.
In this shrine, there is also a sculpture of young Koxinga and his mother [[Tagawa Matsu]].
It is not to be confused with the nearby [[Koxinga Shrine]]
==See also== * [[Chinese ancestral veneration]] * [[Beiji Temple]] * [[Grand Matsu Temple]] * [[Taiwan Confucian Temple]] * [[State Temple of the Martial God]] * [[Temple of the Five Concubines]] * [[List of temples in Taiwan]] * [[List of tourist attractions in Taiwan]]

[[Image:JapaneseKoxinga.JPG|right|thumb|200px|[[Tagawa Matsu|Koxinga's mother]] and him as a child]]
'''Koxinga Ancestral Shrine''' ({{zh|c=鄭成功祖廟|p=Zhèngchénggōng Zǔmiào}}) is a family [[shrine]] built in [[West Central District]], [[Tainan]], [[Taiwan]] in 1663 by [[Zheng Jing]], to worship his father [[Koxinga]].
When Taiwan became [[Taiwan under Qing rule|part of]] the [[Qing dynasty]], it was renamed "The Cheng's Ancestral Shrine" (鄭氏大宗祠) and today the official name is "Ancestral Shrine of Koxinga". The complex is traditional and elegant. There is an old well in front of the gate and this is all that remains of the original shrine.
The central hall worships the statue of Koxinga as well as the [[spirit tablets]] of each generation of ancestors. In 1771, there was a famous wooden tablet with the character "Three Generations Heritage" (三圭世錫) to prize the virtue of Koxinga's family.
In this shrine, there is also a sculpture of young Koxinga and his mother [[Tagawa Matsu]].
It is not to be confused with the nearby [[Koxinga Shrine]]

[[Image:JapaneseKoxinga.JPG|right|thumb|200px|[[Tagawa Matsu|Koxinga's mother]] and him as a child]]
'''Koxinga Ancestral Shrine''' ({{zh|c=鄭成功祖廟|p=Zhèngchénggōng Zǔmiào}}) is a family [[shrine]] built in [[West Central District]], [[Tainan]], [[Taiwan]] in 1663 by [[Zheng Jing]], to worship his father [[Koxinga]].
When Taiwan became [[Taiwan under Qing rule|part of]] the [[Qing dynasty]], it was renamed "The Cheng's Ancestral Shrine" (鄭氏大宗祠) and today the official name is "Ancestral Shrine of Koxinga". The complex is traditional and elegant. There is an old well in front of the gate and this is all that remains of the original shrine.
The central hall worships the statue of Koxinga as well as the [[spirit tablets]] of each generation of ancestors. In 1771, there was a famous wooden tablet with the character "Three Generations Heritage" (三圭世錫) to prize the virtue of Koxinga's family.
In this shrine, there is also a sculpture of young Koxinga and his mother [[Tagawa Matsu]].
It is not to be confused with the nearby [[Koxinga Shrine]]
==See also== * [[Chinese ancestral veneration]] * [[Beiji Temple]] * [[Grand Matsu Temple]] * [[Taiwan Confucian Temple]] * [[State Temple of the Martial God]] * [[Temple of the Five Concubines]] * [[List of temples in Taiwan]] * [[List of tourist attractions in Taiwan]]

The complex is traditional and elegant. There is an old well in front of the gate and this is all that remains of the original shrine.
The central hall worships the statue of Koxinga as well as the [[spirit tablets]] of each generation of ancestors. In 1771, there was a famous wooden tablet with the character "Three Generations Heritage" (三圭世錫) to prize the virtue of Koxinga's family.
In this shrine, there is also a sculpture of young Koxinga and his mother [[:en:Tagawa Matsu|Tagawa Matsu]].
It is not to be confused with the nearby [[Koxinga Shrine]]
==See also== * [[:en:Chinese ancestral veneration|Chinese ancestral veneration]] * [[:en:Beiji Temple|Beiji Temple]] * [[:en:Grand Matsu Temple|Grand Matsu Temple]] * [[Taiwan Confucian Temple]] * [[State Temple of the Martial God]] * [[Temple of the Five Concubines]] * [[:en:List of temples in Taiwan|List of temples in Taiwan]] * [[List of tourist attractions in Taiwan]]

[[Image:JapaneseKoxinga.JPG|right|thumb|200px|[[:en:Tagawa Matsu|Koxinga's mother]] and him as a child]]
'''Koxinga Ancestral Shrine''' ({{zh|c=鄭成功祖廟|p=Zhèngchénggōng Zǔmiào}}) is a family [[shrine]] built in [[:en:West Central District|West Central District]], [[:en:Tainan|Tainan]], [[:en:Taiwan|Taiwan]] in 1663 by [[:en:Zheng Jing|Zheng Jing]], to worship his father [[:en:Koxinga|Koxinga]].
When Taiwan became [[:en:Taiwan under Qing rule|part of]] the [[:en:Qing dynasty|Qing dynasty]], it was renamed "The Cheng's Ancestral Shrine" (鄭氏大宗祠) and today the official name is "Ancestral Shrine of Koxinga". The complex is traditional and elegant. There is an old well in front of the gate and this is all that remains of the original shrine.
The central hall worships the statue of Koxinga as well as the [[spirit tablets]] of each generation of ancestors. In 1771, there was a famous wooden tablet with the character "Three Generations Heritage" (三圭世錫) to prize the virtue of Koxinga's family.
In this shrine, there is also a sculpture of young Koxinga and his mother [[:en:Tagawa Matsu|Tagawa Matsu]].
It is not to be confused with the nearby [[Koxinga Shrine]]
==See also== * [[:en:Chinese ancestral veneration|Chinese ancestral veneration]] * [[:en:Beiji Temple|Beiji Temple]] * [[:en:Grand Matsu Temple|Grand Matsu Temple]] * [[:en:Taiwan Confucian Temple|Taiwan Confucian Temple]] * [[State Temple of the Martial God]] * [[:en:Temple of the Five Concubines|Temple of the Five Concubines]] * [[:en:List of temples in Taiwan|List of temples in Taiwan]] * [[List of tourist attractions in Taiwan]]

[[Image:JapaneseKoxinga.JPG|right|thumb|200px|[[:en:Tagawa Matsu|Koxinga's mother]] and him as a child]]
'''Koxinga Ancestral Shrine''' ({{zh|c=鄭成功祖廟|p=Zhèngchénggōng Zǔmiào}}) is a family [[shrine]] built in [[:en:West Central District|West Central District]], [[:en:Tainan|Tainan]], [[:en:Taiwan|Taiwan]] in 1663 by [[:en:Zheng Jing|Zheng Jing]], to worship his father [[:en:Koxinga|Koxinga]].
When Taiwan became [[:en:Taiwan under Qing rule|part of]] the [[:en:Qing dynasty|Qing dynasty]], it was renamed "The Cheng's Ancestral Shrine" (鄭氏大宗祠) and today the official name is "Ancestral Shrine of Koxinga". The complex is traditional and elegant. There is an old well in front of the gate and this is all that remains of the original shrine.
The central hall worships the statue of Koxinga as well as the [[:en:spirit tablets|spirit tablets]] of each generation of ancestors. In 1771, there was a famous wooden tablet with the character "Three Generations Heritage" (三圭世錫) to prize the virtue of Koxinga's family.
In this shrine, there is also a sculpture of young Koxinga and his mother [[:en:Tagawa Matsu|Tagawa Matsu]].
It is not to be confused with the nearby [[Koxinga Shrine]]

[[Image:JapaneseKoxinga.JPG|right|thumb|200px|[[:en:Tagawa Matsu|Koxinga's mother]] and him as a child]]
'''Koxinga Ancestral Shrine''' ({{zh|c=鄭成功祖廟|p=Zhèngchénggōng Zǔmiào}}) is a family [[shrine]] built in [[:en:West Central District|West Central District]], [[:en:Tainan|Tainan]], [[:en:Taiwan|Taiwan]] in 1663 by [[:en:Zheng Jing|Zheng Jing]], to worship his father [[:en:Koxinga|Koxinga]].
When Taiwan became [[:en:Taiwan under Qing rule|part of]] the [[:en:Qing dynasty|Qing dynasty]], it was renamed "The Cheng's Ancestral Shrine" (鄭氏大宗祠) and today the official name is "Ancestral Shrine of Koxinga". The complex is traditional and elegant. There is an old well in front of the gate and this is all that remains of the original shrine.
The central hall worships the statue of Koxinga as well as the [[:en:spirit tablets|spirit tablets]] of each generation of ancestors. In 1771, there was a famous wooden tablet with the character "Three Generations Heritage" (三圭世錫) to prize the virtue of Koxinga's family.
In this shrine, there is also a sculpture of young Koxinga and his mother [[:en:Tagawa Matsu|Tagawa Matsu]].
It is not to be confused with the nearby [[Koxinga Shrine]]
==See also== * [[:en:Chinese ancestral veneration|Chinese ancestral veneration]] * [[:en:Beiji Temple|Beiji Temple]] * [[:en:Grand Matsu Temple|Grand Matsu Temple]] * [[:en:Taiwan Confucian Temple|Taiwan Confucian Temple]] * [[:en:State Temple of the Martial God|State Temple of the Martial God]] * [[:en:Temple of the Five Concubines|Temple of the Five Concubines]] * [[:en:List of temples in Taiwan|List of temples in Taiwan]] * [[List of tourist attractions in Taiwan]]

[[Image:JapaneseKoxinga.JPG|right|thumb|200px|[[:en:Tagawa Matsu|Koxinga's mother]] and him as a child]]
'''Koxinga Ancestral Shrine''' ({{zh|c=鄭成功祖廟|p=Zhèngchénggōng Zǔmiào}}) is a family [[shrine]] built in [[:en:West Central District|West Central District]], [[:en:Tainan|Tainan]], [[:en:Taiwan|Taiwan]] in 1663 by [[:en:Zheng Jing|Zheng Jing]], to worship his father [[:en:Koxinga|Koxinga]].
When Taiwan became [[:en:Taiwan under Qing rule|part of]] the [[:en:Qing dynasty|Qing dynasty]], it was renamed "The Cheng's Ancestral Shrine" (鄭氏大宗祠) and today the official name is "Ancestral Shrine of Koxinga". The complex is traditional and elegant. There is an old well in front of the gate and this is all that remains of the original shrine.
The central hall worships the statue of Koxinga as well as the [[:en:spirit tablets|spirit tablets]] of each generation of ancestors. In 1771, there was a famous wooden tablet with the character "Three Generations Heritage" (三圭世錫) to prize the virtue of Koxinga's family.
In this shrine, there is also a sculpture of young Koxinga and his mother [[:en:Tagawa Matsu|Tagawa Matsu]].
It is not to be confused with the nearby [[Koxinga Shrine]]
==See also== * [[:en:Chinese ancestral veneration|Chinese ancestral veneration]] * [[:en:Beiji Temple|Beiji Temple]] * [[:en:Grand Matsu Temple|Grand Matsu Temple]] * [[:en:Taiwan Confucian Temple|Taiwan Confucian Temple]] * [[:en:State Temple of the Martial God|State Temple of the Martial God]] * [[:en:Temple of the Five Concubines|Temple of the Five Concubines]] * [[:en:List of temples in Taiwan|List of temples in Taiwan]] * [[List of tourist attractions in Taiwan]]

[[Image:JapaneseKoxinga.JPG|right|thumb|200px|[[:en:Tagawa Matsu|Koxinga's mother]] and him as a child]]
'''Koxinga Ancestral Shrine''' ({{zh|c=鄭成功祖廟|p=Zhèngchénggōng Zǔmiào}}) is a family [[shrine]] built in [[:en:West Central District|West Central District]], [[:en:Tainan|Tainan]], [[:en:Taiwan|Taiwan]] in 1663 by [[:en:Zheng Jing|Zheng Jing]], to worship his father [[:en:Koxinga|Koxinga]].
When Taiwan became [[:en:Taiwan under Qing rule|part of]] the [[:en:Qing dynasty|Qing dynasty]], it was renamed "The Cheng's Ancestral Shrine" (鄭氏大宗祠) and today the official name is "Ancestral Shrine of Koxinga". The complex is traditional and elegant. There is an old well in front of the gate and this is all that remains of the original shrine.
The central hall worships the statue of Koxinga as well as the [[:en:spirit tablets|spirit tablets]] of each generation of ancestors. In 1771, there was a famous wooden tablet with the character "Three Generations Heritage" (三圭世錫) to prize the virtue of Koxinga's family.
In this shrine, there is also a sculpture of young Koxinga and his mother [[:en:Tagawa Matsu|Tagawa Matsu]].
It is not to be confused with the nearby [[Koxinga Shrine]]
==See also== * [[:en:Chinese ancestral veneration|Chinese ancestral veneration]] * [[:en:Beiji Temple|Beiji Temple]] * [[:en:Grand Matsu Temple|Grand Matsu Temple]] * [[:en:Taiwan Confucian Temple|Taiwan Confucian Temple]] * [[:en:State Temple of the Martial God|State Temple of the Martial God]] * [[:en:Temple of the Five Concubines|Temple of the Five Concubines]] * [[:en:List of temples in Taiwan|List of temples in Taiwan]] * [[List of tourist attractions in Taiwan]]

[[Image:JapaneseKoxinga.JPG|right|thumb|200px|[[:en:Tagawa Matsu|Koxinga's mother]] and him as a child]]
'''Koxinga Ancestral Shrine''' ({{zh|c=鄭成功祖廟|p=Zhèngchénggōng Zǔmiào}}) is a family [[shrine]] built in [[:en:West Central District|West Central District]], [[:en:Tainan|Tainan]], [[:en:Taiwan|Taiwan]] in 1663 by [[:en:Zheng Jing|Zheng Jing]], to worship his father [[:en:Koxinga|Koxinga]].
When Taiwan became [[:en:Taiwan under Qing rule|part of]] the [[:en:Qing dynasty|Qing dynasty]], it was renamed "The Cheng's Ancestral Shrine" (鄭氏大宗祠) and today the official name is "Ancestral Shrine of Koxinga". The complex is traditional and elegant. There is an old well in front of the gate and this is all that remains of the original shrine.
The central hall worships the statue of Koxinga as well as the [[:en:spirit tablets|spirit tablets]] of each generation of ancestors. In 1771, there was a famous wooden tablet with the character "Three Generations Heritage" (三圭世錫) to prize the virtue of Koxinga's family.
In this shrine, there is also a sculpture of young Koxinga and his mother [[:en:Tagawa Matsu|Tagawa Matsu]].
It is not to be confused with the nearby [[Koxinga Shrine]]
==See also== * [[:en:Chinese ancestral veneration|Chinese ancestral veneration]] * [[:en:Beiji Temple|Beiji Temple]] * [[:en:Grand Matsu Temple|Grand Matsu Temple]] * [[:en:Taiwan Confucian Temple|Taiwan Confucian Temple]] * [[:en:State Temple of the Martial God|State Temple of the Martial God]] * [[:en:Temple of the Five Concubines|Temple of the Five Concubines]] * [[:en:List of temples in Taiwan|List of temples in Taiwan]] * [[List of tourist attractions in Taiwan]]

[[Image:JapaneseKoxinga.JPG|right|thumb|200px|{{ill|Tagawa Matsu|en|Tagawa Matsu|WD=Q699779|lt=Koxinga's mother}} and him as a child]]
'''Koxinga Ancestral Shrine''' ({{zh|c=鄭成功祖廟|p=Zhèngchénggōng Zǔmiào}}) is a family [[shrine]] built in {{ill|West Central District|en|West Central District|WD=Q706699|lt=West Central District}}, {{ill|Tainan|en|Tainan|WD=Q140631|lt=Tainan}}, {{ill|Taiwan|en|Taiwan|WD=Q865|lt=Taiwan}} in 1663 by {{ill|Zheng Jing|en|Zheng Jing|WD=Q84018|lt=Zheng Jing}}, to worship his father {{ill|Koxinga|en|Koxinga|WD=Q197615|lt=Koxinga}}.
When Taiwan became {{ill|Taiwan under Qing rule|en|Taiwan under Qing rule|WD=Q391189|lt=part of}} the {{ill|Qing dynasty|en|Qing dynasty|WD=Q8733|lt=Qing dynasty}}, it was renamed "The Cheng's Ancestral Shrine" (鄭氏大宗祠) and today the official name is "Ancestral Shrine of Koxinga". The complex is traditional and elegant. There is an old well in front of the gate and this is all that remains of the original shrine.
The central hall worships the statue of Koxinga as well as the [[:en:spirit tablets|spirit tablets]] of each generation of ancestors. In 1771, there was a famous wooden tablet with the character "Three Generations Heritage" (三圭世錫) to prize the virtue of Koxinga's family.
In this shrine, there is also a sculpture of young Koxinga and his mother {{ill|Tagawa Matsu|en|Tagawa Matsu|WD=Q699779|lt=Tagawa Matsu}}.
It is not to be confused with the nearby [[Koxinga Shrine]]
==See also== * [[:en:Chinese ancestral veneration|Chinese ancestral veneration]] * {{ill|Beiji Temple|en|Beiji Temple|WD=Q15911223|lt=Beiji Temple}} * {{ill|Grand Matsu Temple|en|Grand Matsu Temple|WD=Q10934707|lt=Grand Matsu Temple}} * [[:en:Taiwan Confucian Temple|Taiwan Confucian Temple]] * {{ill|State Temple of the Martial God|en|State Temple of the Martial God|WD=Q15079274|lt=State Temple of the Martial God}} * {{ill|Temple of the Five Concubines|en|Temple of the Five Concubines|WD=Q7698759|lt=Temple of the Five Concubines}} * {{ill|List of temples in Taiwan|en|List of temples in Taiwan|WD=Q106283273|lt=List of temples in Taiwan}} * [[List of tourist attractions in Taiwan]]

[[Image:JapaneseKoxinga.JPG|right|thumb|200px|{{ill|Tagawa Matsu|en|Tagawa Matsu|WD=Q699779|lt=Koxinga's mother}} and him as a child]]
'''Koxinga Ancestral Shrine''' ({{zh|c=鄭成功祖廟|p=Zhèngchénggōng Zǔmiào}}) is a family [[shrine]] built in {{ill|West Central District|en|West Central District|WD=Q706699|lt=West Central District}}, {{ill|Tainan|en|Tainan|WD=Q140631|lt=Tainan}}, {{ill|Taiwan|en|Taiwan|WD=Q865|lt=Taiwan}} in 1663 by {{ill|Zheng Jing|en|Zheng Jing|WD=Q84018|lt=Zheng Jing}}, to worship his father {{ill|Koxinga|en|Koxinga|WD=Q197615|lt=Koxinga}}.
When Taiwan became {{ill|Taiwan under Qing rule|en|Taiwan under Qing rule|WD=Q391189|lt=part of}} the {{ill|Qing dynasty|en|Qing dynasty|WD=Q8733|lt=Qing dynasty}}, it was renamed "The Cheng's Ancestral Shrine" (鄭氏大宗祠) and today the official name is "Ancestral Shrine of Koxinga". The complex is traditional and elegant. There is an old well in front of the gate and this is all that remains of the original shrine.
The central hall worships the statue of Koxinga as well as the [[:en:spirit tablets|spirit tablets]] of each generation of ancestors. In 1771, there was a famous wooden tablet with the character "Three Generations Heritage" (三圭世錫) to prize the virtue of Koxinga's family.
In this shrine, there is also a sculpture of young Koxinga and his mother {{ill|Tagawa Matsu|en|Tagawa Matsu|WD=Q699779|lt=Tagawa Matsu}}.
It is not to be confused with the nearby [[Koxinga Shrine]]
==See also== * [[:en:Chinese ancestral veneration|Chinese ancestral veneration]] * {{ill|Beiji Temple|en|Beiji Temple|WD=Q15911223|lt=Beiji Temple}} * {{ill|Grand Matsu Temple|en|Grand Matsu Temple|WD=Q10934707|lt=Grand Matsu Temple}} * [[:en:Taiwan Confucian Temple|Taiwan Confucian Temple]] * {{ill|State Temple of the Martial God|en|State Temple of the Martial God|WD=Q15079274|lt=State Temple of the Martial God}} * {{ill|Temple of the Five Concubines|en|Temple of the Five Concubines|WD=Q7698759|lt=Temple of the Five Concubines}} * {{ill|List of temples in Taiwan|en|List of temples in Taiwan|WD=Q106283273|lt=List of temples in Taiwan}} * [[List of tourist attractions in Taiwan]]

{{Infobox building | name = Koxinga Ancestral Shrine | native_name = 鄭成功祖廟 | native_name_lang = zh | logo = | logo_size = | logo_alt = | logo_caption = | image = 鄭氏家廟正門.JPG | image_size = 250px | image_alt = | image_caption = | map_type = | map_alt = | map_caption = | map_size = | map_dot_label = | map_dot_mark = | relief = | former_names = | alternate_names = | etymology = | status = | cancelled = | topped_out = | building_type = [[Shrine]] | architectural_style = | classification = | location = {{ill|West Central District|en|West Central District|WD=Q706699|lt=West Central}}, {{ill|Tainan|en|Tainan|WD=Q140631|lt=Tainan}}, {{ill|Taiwan|en|Taiwan|WD=Q865|lt=Taiwan}} | a11ress = | coordinates = {{coord|22.992597|N|120.203054|E|type:landmark_region:TW|display=inline,title}} | altitude = | current_tenants = | namesake = | groundbreaking_date = | start_date = | stop_date = | est_completion = | topped_out_date = | completion_date = | opened_date = 1663 | inauguration_date = | relocated_date = | renovation_date = | closing_date = | demolition_date = | cost = | ren_cost = | client = | owner = | landlord = | affiliation = | height = | architectural = | tip = | antenna_spire = | roof = | top_floor = | observatory = | diameter = | circumference = | weight = | other_dimensions = | structural_system = | material = | area = | floor_count = | floor_area = | elevator_count = | grounds_area = | architect = | architecture_firm = | developer = {{ill|Zheng Jing|en|Zheng Jing|WD=Q84018|lt=Zheng Jing}} | engineer = | structural_engineer = | services_engineer = | civil_engineer = | other_designers = | quantity_surveyor = | main_contractor = | awards = | designations = | known_for = | ren_architect = | ren_firm = | ren_engineer = | ren_str_engineer = | ren_serv_engineer = | ren_civ_engineer = | ren_oth_designers = | ren_qty_surveyor = | ren_contractor = | ren_awards = | rooms = | parking = | website = | embed = | embe11ed = | references = | footnotes = }}
[[Image:JapaneseKoxinga.JPG|right|thumb|200px|{{ill|Tagawa Matsu|en|Tagawa Matsu|WD=Q699779|lt=Koxinga's mother}} and him as a child]]
'''Koxinga Ancestral Shrine''' ({{zh|c=鄭成功祖廟|p=Zhèngchénggōng Zǔmiào}}) is a family [[shrine]] built in {{ill|West Central District|en|West Central District|WD=Q706699|lt=West Central District}}, {{ill|Tainan|en|Tainan|WD=Q140631|lt=Tainan}}, {{ill|Taiwan|en|Taiwan|WD=Q865|lt=Taiwan}} in 1663 by {{ill|Zheng Jing|en|Zheng Jing|WD=Q84018|lt=Zheng Jing}}, to worship his father {{ill|Koxinga|en|Koxinga|WD=Q197615|lt=Koxinga}}.
When Taiwan became {{ill|Taiwan under Qing rule|en|Taiwan under Qing rule|WD=Q391189|lt=part of}} the {{ill|Qing dynasty|en|Qing dynasty|WD=Q8733|lt=Qing dynasty}}, it was renamed "The Cheng's Ancestral Shrine" (鄭氏大宗祠) and today the official name is "Ancestral Shrine of Koxinga". The complex is traditional and elegant. There is an old well in front of the gate and this is all that remains of the original shrine.
The central hall worships the statue of Koxinga as well as the [[:en:spirit tablets|spirit tablets]] of each generation of ancestors. In 1771, there was a famous wooden tablet with the character "Three Generations Heritage" (三圭世錫) to prize the virtue of Koxinga's family.
In this shrine, there is also a sculpture of young Koxinga and his mother {{ill|Tagawa Matsu|en|Tagawa Matsu|WD=Q699779|lt=Tagawa Matsu}}.
It is not to be confused with the nearby [[Koxinga Shrine]]
==See also== * [[:en:Chinese ancestral veneration|Chinese ancestral veneration]] * {{ill|Beiji Temple|en|Beiji Temple|WD=Q15911223|lt=Beiji Temple}} * {{ill|Grand Matsu Temple|en|Grand Matsu Temple|WD=Q10934707|lt=Grand Matsu Temple}} * [[:en:Taiwan Confucian Temple|Taiwan Confucian Temple]] * {{ill|State Temple of the Martial God|en|State Temple of the Martial God|WD=Q15079274|lt=State Temple of the Martial God}} * {{ill|Temple of the Five Concubines|en|Temple of the Five Concubines|WD=Q7698759|lt=Temple of the Five Concubines}} * {{ill|List of temples in Taiwan|en|List of temples in Taiwan|WD=Q106283273|lt=List of temples in Taiwan}} * [[List of tourist attractions in Taiwan]]

The central hall worships the statue of Koxinga as well as the [[:en:spirit tablets|spirit tablets]] of each generation of ancestors. In 1771, there was a famous wooden tablet with the character "Three Generations Heritage" (三圭世錫) to prize the virtue of Koxinga's family.
In this shrine, there is also a sculpture of young Koxinga and his mother {{ill|Tagawa Matsu|en|Tagawa Matsu|WD=Q699779|lt=Tagawa Matsu}}.
It is not to be confused with the nearby [[Koxinga Shrine]]

[[Image:JapaneseKoxinga.JPG|right|thumb|200px|{{ill|Tagawa Matsu|en|Tagawa Matsu|WD=Q699779|lt=Koxinga's mother|1=Tagawa Matsu}} and him as a child]]
'''Koxinga Ancestral Shrine''' ({{zh|c=鄭成功祖廟|p=Zhèngchénggōng Zǔmiào}}) is a family [[shrine]] built in {{ill|West Central District|en|West Central District|WD=Q706699|lt=West Central District|1=West Central District}}, {{ill|Tainan|en|Tainan|WD=Q140631|lt=Tainan|1=Tainan}}, {{ill|Taiwan|en|Taiwan|WD=Q865|lt=Taiwan|1=Taiwan}} in 1663 by {{ill|Zheng Jing|en|Zheng Jing|WD=Q84018|lt=Zheng Jing|1=Zheng Jing}}, to worship his father {{ill|Koxinga|en|Koxinga|WD=Q197615|lt=Koxinga|1=Koxinga}}.
When Taiwan became {{ill|Taiwan under Qing rule|en|Taiwan under Qing rule|WD=Q391189|lt=part of|1=Taiwan under Qing rule}} the {{ill|Qing dynasty|en|Qing dynasty|WD=Q8733|lt=Qing dynasty|1=Qing dynasty}}, it was renamed "The Cheng's Ancestral Shrine" (鄭氏大宗祠) and today the official name is "Ancestral Shrine of Koxinga". The complex is traditional and elegant. There is an old well in front of the gate and this is all that remains of the original shrine.
The central hall worships the statue of Koxinga as well as the [[:en:spirit tablets|spirit tablets]] of each generation of ancestors. In 1771, there was a famous wooden tablet with the character "Three Generations Heritage" (三圭世錫) to prize the virtue of Koxinga's family.
In this shrine, there is also a sculpture of young Koxinga and his mother {{ill|Tagawa Matsu|en|Tagawa Matsu|WD=Q699779|lt=Tagawa Matsu|1=Tagawa Matsu}}.
It is not to be confused with the nearby [[Koxinga Shrine]]
==See also== * [[:en:Chinese ancestral veneration|Chinese ancestral veneration]] * {{ill|Beiji Temple|en|Beiji Temple|WD=Q15911223|lt=Beiji Temple|1=Beiji Temple}} * {{ill|Grand Matsu Temple|en|Grand Matsu Temple|WD=Q10934707|lt=Grand Matsu Temple|1=Grand Matsu Temple}} * [[:en:Taiwan Confucian Temple|Taiwan Confucian Temple]] * {{ill|State Temple of the Martial God|en|State Temple of the Martial God|WD=Q15079274|lt=State Temple of the Martial God|1=State Temple of the Martial God}} * {{ill|Temple of the Five Concubines|en|Temple of the Five Concubines|WD=Q7698759|lt=Temple of the Five Concubines|1=Temple of the Five Concubines}} * {{ill|List of temples in Taiwan|en|List of temples in Taiwan|WD=Q106283273|lt=List of temples in Taiwan|1=List of temples in Taiwan}} * [[List of tourist attractions in Taiwan]]

[[Image:JapaneseKoxinga.JPG|right|thumb|200px|{{ill|Tagawa Matsu|en|Tagawa Matsu|WD=Q699779|lt=Koxinga's mother|1=Tagawa Matsu}} and him as a child]]
'''Koxinga Ancestral Shrine''' ({{zh|c=鄭成功祖廟|p=Zhèngchénggōng Zǔmiào}}) is a family [[shrine]] built in {{ill|West Central District|en|West Central District|WD=Q706699|lt=West Central District|1=West Central District}}, {{ill|Tainan|en|Tainan|WD=Q140631|lt=Tainan|1=Tainan}}, {{ill|Taiwan|en|Taiwan|WD=Q865|lt=Taiwan|1=Taiwan}} in 1663 by {{ill|Zheng Jing|en|Zheng Jing|WD=Q84018|lt=Zheng Jing|1=Zheng Jing}}, to worship his father {{ill|Koxinga|en|Koxinga|WD=Q197615|lt=Koxinga|1=Koxinga}}.
When Taiwan became {{ill|Taiwan under Qing rule|en|Taiwan under Qing rule|WD=Q391189|lt=part of|1=Taiwan under Qing rule}} the {{ill|Qing dynasty|en|Qing dynasty|WD=Q8733|lt=Qing dynasty|1=Qing dynasty}}, it was renamed "The Cheng's Ancestral Shrine" (鄭氏大宗祠) and today the official name is "Ancestral Shrine of Koxinga". The complex is traditional and elegant. There is an old well in front of the gate and this is all that remains of the original shrine.
The central hall worships the statue of Koxinga as well as the [[:en:spirit tablets|spirit tablets]] of each generation of ancestors. In 1771, there was a famous wooden tablet with the character "Three Generations Heritage" (三圭世錫) to prize the virtue of Koxinga's family.
In this shrine, there is also a sculpture of young Koxinga and his mother {{ill|Tagawa Matsu|en|Tagawa Matsu|WD=Q699779|lt=Tagawa Matsu|1=Tagawa Matsu}}.
It is not to be confused with the nearby [[Koxinga Shrine]]
==See also== * [[:en:Chinese ancestral veneration|Chinese ancestral veneration]] * {{ill|Beiji Temple|en|Beiji Temple|WD=Q15911223|lt=Beiji Temple|1=Beiji Temple}} * {{ill|Grand Matsu Temple|en|Grand Matsu Temple|WD=Q10934707|lt=Grand Matsu Temple|1=Grand Matsu Temple}} * [[:en:Taiwan Confucian Temple|Taiwan Confucian Temple]] * {{ill|State Temple of the Martial God|en|State Temple of the Martial God|WD=Q15079274|lt=State Temple of the Martial God|1=State Temple of the Martial God}} * {{ill|Temple of the Five Concubines|en|Temple of the Five Concubines|WD=Q7698759|lt=Temple of the Five Concubines|1=Temple of the Five Concubines}} * {{ill|List of temples in Taiwan|en|List of temples in Taiwan|WD=Q106283273|lt=List of temples in Taiwan|1=List of temples in Taiwan}} * [[List of tourist attractions in Taiwan]]
==External links== * [https://web.archive.org/web/20070927230240/http://tour.tncg.gov.tw/english/historic/ht01_1.asp?highspot_sn=534 Tainan City travel information]
